parentQuestion: A patient wakes up unable to extend the wrist or fingers after falling asleep wi...

scaffolds.q: The radial nerve controls extension of the wrist and fingers.
scaffolds.explain: The radial nerve innervates all the extensor muscles of the posterior forearm — the muscles that lift your wrist and straighten your fingers. Lose the radial nerve, and your hand drops into flexion. You literally can't lift your wrist. I want you to appreciate the elegance of this single nerve controlling ALL extension...

scaffolds.q: Prolonged pressure on a nerve can cause temporary paralysis.
scaffolds.explain: Sustained compression cuts off blood flow to the nerve (ischemia) and causes mechanical deformation of nerve fibers. The result is temporary loss of function — neurapraxia. Most cases recover within weeks to months once pressure is relieved. But severe or prolonged compression? Permanent damage. Breathe in this knowledge:drawn into this vulnerability — hours of pressure, and the nerve stops working...
